title: Local stars sparkle at The Choice Talent Show
description: GEORGE NEWS - The final round of The Choice...
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE NEWS - The final round of The Choice Talent Show last weekend was nothing short of spectacular, leaving no doubt that the Garden Route is home to some of South Africa's finest young talent.


> The semi-finals, held on 5 April, featured over 60 participants competing across three categories: dance, drama and singing.


The top seven finalists in each category were selected for the grand finale last Saturday, 26 April.

Gallery: Local stars sparkle at The Choice Talent Show

The Garden Route Mall, in partnership with White Peacock Studio, hosted this talent show. The atmosphere was electric as emerging performers captivated audiences with their passion and skill.

The winners in each category are Rylan Lemmetuis (13, singing), Kirsten Pharoah (15, drama) and Savannah de Jongh (19, dance).

Each of the winners walked away with an impressive prize package that included R5 000 in cash, R5 000 in Garden Route Mall vouchers, and a R300 McDonald's voucher.

Rylan Lemmetuis (13), winner of the singing chategory. Photo: Ashwin Manus

The winner of the drama category, Kirsten Pharoah (15), receives her cheque. Photo: Ashwin Manus


> Quintin van Biljon, founder and principal of White Peacock Studio, said this event not only showcased raw, local talent, but also proved the importance of creating platforms for young performers to shine. "We're incredibly proud of every-one who participated," he said.


Production manager Quintin van Biljon, the founding member of White Peacock Studio. Photo: Ashwin Manus

Andy and Friends on stage. Photo: Michelle Pienaar

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
